channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean Parliament votes to impeach acting president
item.description: The South Korean Parliament voted to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo, temporarily stripping him of power less than two weeks after the same body voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol following his brief declaration of martial law that threw the country into a political crisis.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The Chinese government has placed sanctions on seven companies in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital after forcibly removing staff and patients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran regime under 'immense pressure' amid incoming Trump admin policies, regional losses, economic woes
item.description: As a result of the Iranian regime’s financing of Islamist terrorist organization and its economic corruption, the people of the Mideast nation are facing widespread gas and power outages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly has voted to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leaders and politicians pay homage to India’s former prime minister, Manmohan Singh
item.description: Authorities in India have declared a seven-day mourning period to honor Manmohan Singh as politicians and public paid tributes to the country’s former prime minister widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's military intelligence says North Korean troops are suffering heavy battlefield losses
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — North Korean troops are suffering heavy losses in the fighting in Russia's Kursk region and facing logistical difficulties as a result of Ukrainian attacks, Ukraine’s military intelligence said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is known about a plane crash in Kazakhstan as experts suspect Russian air defense fire
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday an Azerbaijani airliner that crashed in Kazakhstan most likely went down after being hit by Russian air defense systems.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden arrives in St. Croix for last vacation as president
item.description: President Biden on Thursday arrived in St. Croix for his last vacation as president before leaving office next month. The president left the White House the day after Christmas with first lady Jill Biden. He arrived in St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands around 2 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: NATO calls for investigation into Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: NATO called for a formal investigation Thursday into the Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people in Kazakhstan. “Our thoughts and prayers are with the families and victims of #AzerbaijanAirlines flight #J28243.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Grandson of former commandant of Auschwitz on rise of antisemitism, his life as a pastor
item.description: Kai Höss, the grandson of former Auschwitz Commandant Rudolf Höss, speaks with Fox News Digital about antisemitism today and how he reconciles his Christian faith with his grandfather's past.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prayers and tears mark 20 years since the Indian Ocean tsunami that killed some 230,000 people
item.description: BANDA ACEH, Indonesia (AP) — People gathered in prayer and visited mass graves in Indonesia’s Aceh province on Thursday to mark 20 years since the massive Indian Ocean tsunami hit the region in one of modern history’s worst natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: REZA SAIFULLAH and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Presidential candidate in Taiwan, Ko Wen-je, indicted for corruption
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Prosecutors in Taiwan indicted former presidential candidate and Taiwan People's Party founder Ko Wen-je on corruption charges Thursday, accusing him of taking bribes during his time as mayor of the island's capital.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia being blamed for Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed hundreds of miles off course, killing dozens
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shaheen breaks glass ceiling on Foreign Relations Committee
item.description: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen (D-N.H.) insists the Senate can chart a bipartisan course on global challenges under President-elect Trump, despite the Republican leader demonstrating his disruptive influence on the party even before reentering the White House.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: US military carries out airstrike in Syria, killing 2 ISIS operatives
item.description: The U.S. military conducted an airstrike on Monday in Syria, where they killed a pair of ISIS operatives, wounded another and destroyed a truckload of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
